mnemonics.title: Three-Way Handshake
mnemonics.content: SYN -> SYN-ACK -> ACK: 'SYNTAX - Clients Start Asking Questions' - Client SYN, Server SYN-ACK, Client ACK

mnemonics.title: TCP Flags
mnemonics.content: Flags: 'SYN FIN RST PSH ACK URG' - Sequence: 'SFlag Runs Promptly And Usually' (use first letters)

mnemonics.title: Connection States
mnemonics.content: Establishment: LISTEN -> SYN_RCVD -> ESTABLISHED. Termination: FIN_WAIT_1 -> FIN_WAIT_2 -> TIME_WAIT -> CLOSED

mnemonics.title: TCP Header Minimum Size
mnemonics.content: 20 bytes = 5 words (each word = 4 bytes). Remember: '20 bytes = 5 words, options can add 40 more'

acronyms.usage: Largest data payload in a single TCP segment
Flag to initiate TCP connection (three-way handshake)
Flag to immediately push data to application
Not TCP-specific but related to port translation

visualTips.visualization: Draw three boxes: Client -> SYN -> Server; Server -> SYN-ACK -> Client; Client -> ACK -> Server. Number each step 1,2,3. Show sequence numbers incrementing.
Draw horizontal line with states. Use arrows showing: FIN -> ACK -> FIN -> ACK flowing between client and server. Mark TIME_WAIT on client side.
Draw 32-bit row structure. Row 1: SrcPort, DstPort. Row 2: SeqNum. Row 3: AckNum. Row 4: DataOffset+Flags+Window. Row 5: Checksum+UrgentPtr. Row 6+: Options. Color-code each field.
Two columns: TCP with checkmarks (reliable, ordered, flow control); UDP with X marks (unreliable, unordered, no flow). Show connection diagram differences.

analogies.analogy: Like a phone call - you dial (SYN), the other person answers (SYN-ACK), you confirm (ACK), then talk (ESTABLISHED). When done, you say goodbye (FINs) and hang up.
UDP is like sending a letter - you send it but don't know if it arrived or the order it will be delivered. TCP is like registered mail with delivery confirmation.
Like tracking numbers on shipped packages - each byte gets a number so you know exactly what arrived and what might be missing.
Like a conversation where one person says 'wait, I'm still processing' when the other talks too fast - prevents overwhelming the listener.
Like apartment numbers in an apartment building (IP address). Port numbers identify which specific apartment (application) the message is for.
